untranslatable_reason: This signal requires detection of a corporate event (tender offer announcement) with specific price levels and acceptance rates. These are event-driven conditions that cannot be expressed using technical price/volume indicators alone. The strategy depends on: (1) real-time tender offer data not available in price feeds, (2) probabilistic acceptance rate modeling (50%+), and (3) multi-leg execution across tender and secondary market. Technical indicators cannot capture tender offer existence or expiration timing.
translation_notes: Signal ID MCMILLAN_DRY_199 is fundamentally an event arbitrage strategy, not a technical signal. It requires external data feeds for M&A events and tender offer details. The entry condition (buy at market when tender exists above current price) and exit condition (sell remainder at lower reopening price post-expiration) are temporal event-driven, not price-pattern driven. Backtesting would require a custom event database layer, not standard OHLCV indicators.
